content:
We need to make sure the GitHub repository is fully up to date with all current changes in this environment.

Repository:
https://github.com/deadevil2002/heavyar-app.git

IMPORTANT RULES:
- Do NOT modify any application logic
- Do NOT refactor anything
- Do NOT create new features
- Do NOT change UI or structure
- Do NOT run any dev server
- Only detect, commit, and push existing changes

Task:

1. Check the current git status in this environment.

2. Identify:
   - modified files
   - untracked files
   - staged changes
   - any pending commits

3. Stage all relevant project changes.

4. Create one clean commit with this exact message:
   Sync latest Heavyar project state

5. Push the commit to the current branch on GitHub.

6. After pushing, verify:
   - latest commit hash
   - branch name
   - whether local and remote are fully synced

7. Explicitly confirm if these files were included in the push (if they had changes):
   - expo/mocks/users.ts
   - expo/mocks/equipment.ts

8. Ensure:
   - no unrelated files were included
   - no project files were skipped

Return only a short report:
- push status (success / failed)
- commit hash
- list of pushed files
- any skipped files and why

At the end:
Stop all processes.
Do not leave anything running.

Authentication setup:

You will use a GitHub Personal Access Token for authentication.

TOKEN:
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

IMPORTANT:
- Do NOT print or expose the token in logs or output
- Do NOT store it in any file
- Use it only for git authentication (push/pull)

Task:
1. Configure git authentication using the provided token
2. Ensure remote origin uses HTTPS with token authentication
3. Test authentication with a safe command (e.g. git ls-remote)
4. Proceed with the push operation

Security:
- Never echo the token
- Never include it in the final report
